
```{r}
#' Logistic Regression with Publication-Ready Tables (Data Frame Output)
#'
#' @description
#' Performs a binary logistic regression and returns publication-ready tables
#' as data frames (tibbles), allowing direct use with flextable or other tools.
#'
#' @inheritParams logistic_regression_spss_gtsummary
#'
#' @return
#' A list containing:
#' \describe{
#'   \item{model}{glm object}
#'   \item{table1}{Descriptive table (tibble)}
#'   \item{table2}{Regression table (tibble)}
#'   \item{diagnostics}{Model diagnostics}
#' }
#'
#' @export
logistic_regression <- function(
  data,
  target,
  predictors,
  categorical_vars = NULL,
  reference_levels = list(),
  alpha = 0.05,
  add_global_p = TRUE,
  sort_variables = TRUE
) {
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # 0. Vérification des packages
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  required_pkgs <- c("gtsummary", "dplyr", "broom", "rlang", "tibble", "stringr", "tidyr")
  missing_pkgs <- required_pkgs[!vapply(required_pkgs, requireNamespace, logical(1), quietly = TRUE)]
  
  if (length(missing_pkgs) > 0) {
    stop(paste("Missing packages:", paste(missing_pkgs, collapse = ", ")), call. = FALSE)
  }
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # 1. Data prep
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  
  all_cols <- c(target, predictors)
  if (!all(all_cols %in% names(data))) {
    stop("Variables missing in dataset.", call. = FALSE)
  }
  
  df <- data[, all_cols, drop = FALSE]
  df <- stats::na.omit(df)
  
  # Cible en facteur
  df[[target]] <- as.factor(df[[target]])
  if (nlevels(df[[target]]) != 2) {
    stop("Target must be binary.", call. = FALSE)
  }
  
  # Définir le niveau de référence de la cible (le premier niveau est la référence "0")
  df[[target]] <- stats::relevel(df[[target]], ref = levels(df[[target]])[1])
  target_levels <- levels(df[[target]])
  col_neg <- target_levels[1]
  col_pos <- target_levels[2]
  
  # Prédicteurs en facteur si nécessaire
  if (!is.null(categorical_vars)) {
    for (var in categorical_vars) {
      if (var %in% names(df)) df[[var]] <- as.factor(df[[var]])
    }
  }
  
  # Appliquer les niveaux de référence spécifiés
  for (var in names(reference_levels)) {
    if (var %in% names(df)) {
      df[[var]] <- stats::relevel(as.factor(df[[var]]), ref = reference_levels[[var]])
    }
  }
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # 2. TABLE 1 (Descriptive Statistics)
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  
  tbl1 <- gtsummary::tbl_summary(
    df,
    by = !!rlang::sym(target),
    statistic = list(
      gtsummary::all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ± {sd}",
      gtsummary::all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)"
    )
  ) %>%
    gtsummary::add_p() %>%
    gtsummary::add_overall()
  
  table1 <- gtsummary::as_tibble(tbl1)
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # 3. MODEL
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  
  formula_str <- paste(target, "~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + "))
  model <- stats::glm(
    stats::as.formula(formula_str),
    data = df,
    family = stats::binomial
  )
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # 4. TABLE 2 (Consolidated: Descriptive + Model Results)
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  
  # A. Récupérer les stats du modèle (broom)
  tidy_mod <- broom::tidy(
    model,
    conf.int = TRUE,
    conf.level = 1 - alpha
  )
  
  # B. Récupérer les effectifs descriptifs par modalité
  # On crée un tableau descriptif manuel pour avoir le contrôle total
  desc_list <- list()
  
  for (var in predictors) {
    var_data <- df[[var]]
    if (!is.factor(var_data)) var_data <- as.factor(var_data)
    
    levels_var <- levels(var_data)
    n_total_var <- sum(table(var_data, useNA = "no"))
    
    # Déterminer la référence pour cette variable
    ref_level <- levels_var[1] # Par défaut, le premier niveau
    
    for (lvl in levels_var) {
      mask <- var_data == lvl
      n_lvl <- sum(mask)
      
      # Calcul croisé avec la cible
      cross <- table(var_data[mask], df[[target]][mask])
      
      # Récupérer les counts pour chaque niveau de la cible
      n_neg <- ifelse(col_neg %in% colnames(cross), cross[1, col_neg], 0)
      n_pos <- ifelse(col_pos %in% colnames(cross), cross[1, col_pos], 0)
      
      pct_neg <- if (n_lvl > 0) round(100 * n_neg / n_lvl, 1) else 0
      pct_pos <- if (n_lvl > 0) round(100 * n_pos / n_lvl, 1) else 0
      
      # Stats du modèle
      is_ref <- (lvl == ref_level)
      
      if (!is_ref) {
        # Chercher le coefficient dans tidy_mod
        # Le nom est souvent "variablelevel"
        term_name <- paste0(var, lvl)
        # Parfois broom met des points ou autres caractères, on fait une recherche flexible
        matching_row <- tidy_mod %>% 
          dplyr::filter(grepl(paste0("^", var, lvl, "$"), term) | 
                        grepl(paste0("^", var, ".", lvl, "$"), term))
        
        if (nrow(matching_row) > 0) {
          or_val <- exp(matching_row$estimate)
          ci_l <- exp(matching_row$conf.low)
          ci_u <- exp(matching_row$conf.high)
          p_val <- matching_row$p.value
          
          or_ci_str <- sprintf("%.2f [%.2f–%.2f]", or_val, ci_l, ci_u)
          p_str <- if (p_val < 0.001) "<0.001" else sprintf("%.3f", p_val)
        } else {
          or_ci_str <- ""
          p_str <- ""
        }
      } else {
        or_ci_str <- "Réf."
        p_str <- ""
      }
      
      row_data <- tibble::tibble(
        Variable = var,
        Modalite = lvl,
        N_Total = n_total_var,
        N_Neg = n_neg,
        Pct_Neg = paste0(n_neg, " (", pct_neg, "%)"),
        N_Pos = n_pos,
        Pct_Pos = paste0(n_pos, " (", pct_pos, "%)"),
        OR_CI = or_ci_str,
        p_value = p_str,
        is_ref = is_ref
      )
      
      desc_list[[length(desc_list) + 1]] <- row_data
    }
  }
  
  table2 <- dplyr::bind_rows(desc_list)
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # 5. Formatting & Indentation
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  
  table2 <- table2 %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      # Indentation des modalités
      Variable_Display = dplyr::if_else(
        is_ref,
        paste0("**", Variable, "** (n=", N_Total, ")"),
        paste0("   ", Modalite)
      ),
      
      # Formatage des p-values (virgule française si besoin)
      p_value = stringr::str_replace_all(p_value, "\\.", ",")
    )
  
  # Tri par p-value si demandé
  if (sort_variables) {
    table2 <- table2 %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        order_p = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(gsub(",", ".", gsub("<", "", p_value))))
      ) %>%
      dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(is_ref), order_p) %>%
      dplyr::select(-order_p)
  }
  
  # Sélection des colonnes finales pour l'affichage
  table2_final <- table2 %>%
    dplyr::select(
      Variable = Variable_Display,
      Modalite,
      !!col_neg := Pct_Neg,
      !!col_pos := Pct_Pos,
      `ORa (IC 95%)` = OR_CI,
      `p-value` = p_value
    )
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # 6. Diagnostics
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  
  null_dev <- model$null.deviance
  res_dev <- model$deviance
  n <- model$n
  logLik_null <- -null_dev / 2
  logLik_model <- -res_dev / 2
  
  diagnostics <- list(
    AIC = stats::AIC(model),
    BIC = stats::BIC(model),
    Deviance = res_dev,
    R2_CoxSnell = 1 - exp((logLik_null - logLik_model) * (2 / n)),
    R2_Nagelkerke = {
      r2_cs <- 1 - exp((logLik_null - logLik_model) * (2 / n))
      r2_max <- 1 - exp((2 * logLik_null) / n)
      r2_cs / r2_max
    }
  )
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # 7. Output
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  
  list(
    model = model,
    table1 = table1,
    table2 = table2_final,
    diagnostics = diagnostics
  )
}
```
